title: glycosmis pseudosapindoides
intro: Glycosmis pseudosapindoides, also known as the False Soapberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Glycosmis pseudosapindoides include its potential in reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine in the form of extracts and oils.
